title: Indefinite Articles (ein, eine) & Negation (kein)
description: Learn how to say 'a/an' and the most common German trick for saying 'none' or 'not a'.
explanation:
You already know 'the' (der/die/das). But what if you just want 'a' table or 'a' bag? In German, 'a/an' is 'ein' for masculine and neuter, and 'eine' for feminine. Plurals don't get an article for 'a' (just like English: you can't say 'a cars').

The beautiful part? To say 'no' or 'none', just put a 'k' in front of it! ein -> kein, eine -> keine.

tables.title: A/An (Indefinite)
tables.note: Notice how Masculine and Neuter share 'ein'!

deep_notes.title: Nicht vs. Kein
deep_notes.text:
This is a trap many learners fall into! Use 'kein' to negate A NOUN (a person, place, or thing). Use 'nicht' (not) for everything else (verbs, adjectives). 
Correct: Ich habe keine Zeit. (I have no time.)
Wrong: Ich habe nicht Zeit.

examples.explanation: Table is 'der Tisch' (Masculine), so it gets 'ein'.
Bag is 'die Tasche' (Feminine), so we negate it with 'keine'.
Plural! No article translates to just having them.

examples.english: We don’t have children. (We have no children.)

exercises.question: Welcher Satz ist richtig für 'This is a woman'?
exercises.explanation: Frau is feminine (die), so it uses 'eine'.

exercises.hint: Wasser ist ein Nomen (das Wasser). Nutzt du 'nicht' oder 'kein'?
exercises.explanation: You use 'kein' to negate nouns (das Wasser -> kein Wasser).

exercises.question: ACHTUNG! Wie übersetzt man 'I have NO sister' (die Schwester)?
exercises.explanation: Since Schwester is feminine (die), you use 'keine' instead of 'nicht'.

exercises.hint: Maschine ist ein feminines Nomen -> die Maschine.

exercises.question: Wo passt 'ein' NICHT?
exercises.explanation: Feminine nouns need the extra 'e': eine!
